programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: Its the middle of summer and the reptiles at Australia Zoo are active and hungry, especially the 44 alligators who inhabit an entire lagoon. Robert has the job of feeding them all at once

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: When a drunken man wreaks havoc on a lake, the wardens must deal with the aftermath. Warden Mike Boone tracks down the culprits responsible for an illegal dumpsite

programme.title: The Girl Who Talked to Dolphins
programme.desc: Documentary exploring an attempt to teach a dolphin to understand English. In the 1960s, a scientist flooded a house and invited a young woman to live there full time with the animal in the hope that the creature would be able to communicate with her. Featuring contributions from those involved in the experiment, who are speaking about events for the first time

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack celebrates Britains houses and palaces, travelling from the grandeur of Hampton Court in west London to a homely terrace in Beamish in Co Durham. He also visits Cragside, Northumberland, one of the first homes to have electric lighting, and helps restore a 15th-century manor in Kent by demonstrating how plaster used to be made

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury starts the longest section of her journey in the historic town of Richmond, which was singled out by Alfred Wainwright as one of his highlights. From there, miles of flat walking between the Dales and her last National Park await, with the North York Moors offering the broadest views and finest weather of her entire adventure

programme.title: Secrets of Size: Atoms to Supergalaxies
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jim Al-Khalili explores the largest known cosmic structures, including, a vast cloud of solar plasma which surrounds and protects the solar system, the black hole at the centre of the Milky Way, which emits the lowest note ever heard in the cosmos, and the giant arc, a collection of galaxies which spans more than three per cent of the observable universe

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The steeplejack tours unusual places of worship, beginning in Preston with one of Britains tallest spires, and a Saxon church in Durham made from the remains of an abandoned Roman fort. He also visits St Pauls Cathedral and meets stonemasons involved in the restoration of York Minster

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury sets off on the last leg of her journey at the highest and most remote overnight spot of the whole walk. Her end goal is Robin Hoods Bay, but first she must cross the North York Moors, walking through Eskdale and on to the woods of Littlebeck before reaching the cliffs at her final destination, where she reflects on the adventure and the legacy of Alfred Wainwright

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Series examining how the BBC has revealed and interpreted moments in history, as well as looking at changes in research covered in documentary television. In the first edition, Professor Alice Roberts uses 70 years of BBC history archive to uncover how Stonehenge has been explained, argued over and debated by some of Britains leading historians and archaeologists. She looks at how new theories discredited old ones, how astronomers and geologists became involved, and why, even after centuries of study, there is still no definitive answer to the mystery of Stonehenge

programme.title: Julian Barnes: Beyond the Page
programme.desc: Acclaimed and award-winning writer Julian Barnes talks to BBC Culture Editor Katie Razzall about his literary life, his pre-occupation with death, why hes written his final novel and missing his readers as he bids them farewell

programme.title: The Sense of an Ending (2017)
programme.desc: Divorced and retired, aging Londoner and vintage camera shop owner Tony Webster whittles down the solitude of his isolated existence by keeping an affectionate relationship with his ex-wife, Margaret, and by accompanying his pregnant daughter, Susie, to antenatal courses. However, the unexpected arrival of an unsettling letter disrupts the fine balance of things in Tonys orderly life, reconnecting him with his first love from college, Veronica, and the nostalgic, yet clouded memories of a distant past. Drama, starring Jim Broadbent, Charlotte Rampling and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The arts critic interviews the acclaimed author, who won the 2011 Man Booker Prize for his novel The Sense of an Ending. Speaking candidly about love, death, memory and grief, Barnes discusses the loss of his wife to cancer in 2008 - the subject of his latest book, Levels of Life

programme.title: My Garden of a Thousand Bees
programme.desc: The work of Bristol wildlife film-maker Martin Dohrn, who during the Covid-19 pandemic set out to record all the bees he could find in his tiny urban garden. Filming more than 60 different species, from Britains largest bumblebees to scissor bees, the size of a mosquito, Dohrn observes how differences in behaviour set different species apart - and eventually he gets so close he can identify individuals just by looking at them

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The steeplejack tours unusual places of worship, beginning in Preston with one of Britains tallest spires, and a Saxon church in Durham made from the remains of an abandoned Roman fort. He also visits St Pauls Cathedral and meets stonemasons involved in the restoration of York Minster

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury sets off on the last leg of her journey at the highest and most remote overnight spot of the whole walk. Her end goal is Robin Hoods Bay, but first she must cross the North York Moors, walking through Eskdale and on to the woods of Littlebeck before reaching the cliffs at her final destination, where she reflects on the adventure and the legacy of Alfred Wainwright

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Series examining how the BBC has revealed and interpreted moments in history, as well as looking at changes in research covered in documentary television. In the first edition, Professor Alice Roberts uses 70 years of BBC history archive to uncover how Stonehenge has been explained, argued over and debated by some of Britains leading historians and archaeologists. She looks at how new theories discredited old ones, how astronomers and geologists became involved, and why, even after centuries of study, there is still no definitive answer to the mystery of Stonehenge

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on June 25, 1999. Featuring S Club 7, Jamiroquai, NSYNC, Britney Spears, Lit, Adam Rickitt, Melanie G and Vengaboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 2, 1999. Featuring Vengaboys, Whitney Houston, The Three Amigos, Suede, A1, Jennifer Lopez and ATB

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell hosts, featuring performances by Suzi Quatro, Dan Hill, Elvis Costello, the Vibrators, Kate Bush, Earth, Wind and Fire, Gerry Rafferty and the Manhattan Transfer. Plus, dance group Legs and Co. Originally broadcast on March 16, 1978

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Peter Powell introduce the edition first aired on March 24, featuring performances by Sinitta, A-ha, Simon Harris, Wet Wet Wet, Debbie Gibson, Climie Fisher, Iron Maiden, Tiffany, Aswad and Whitney Houston

programme.title: Elkie Brooks at the BBC
programme.desc: The singer marks her 80th birthday by looking back over her career, highlighted by some of her finest performances at the BBC, including appearances on Top of the Pops, Parkinson, The Old Grey Whistle Test, Wogan and The One Show. Songs include Pearls a Singer, No More the Fool, Lilac Wine and Fool If You Think Its Over, and in between, Elkie remembers working alongside the likes of Chris Rea and Robert Palmer, and reveals personal insights into what has inspired her

programme.title: Elkie Brooks: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: Annie Nightingale introduces Elkie Brooks at the Hippodrome, Golders Green in 1977. The concert was originally broadcast as part of the BBC Sight and Sound in Concert series, in which music shows were broadcast simultaneously on TV and radio, allowing viewers to enjoy better sound from their hi-fi speakers

programme.title: Elkie Brooks in Concert
programme.desc: The singer performs in this two-part TV special recorded at the Birmingham NEC, producing a subtle blend of rock numbers and gentle ballads. Hits include Fool if You Think its Over and Pearls a Singer. First broadcast August 19 1987

programme.title: Elkie Brooks in Concert
programme.desc: In the second part of this TV concert recorded at Birmingham NEC, the singer performs hits including Lilac Wine, Dont Cry Out Loud and Gasoline Alley. First broadcast August 21 1987

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on June 25, 1999. Featuring S Club 7, Jamiroquai, NSYNC, Britney Spears, Lit, Adam Rickitt, Melanie G and Vengaboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 2, 1999. Featuring Vengaboys, Whitney Houston, The Three Amigos, Suede, A1, Jennifer Lopez and ATB

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell hosts, featuring performances by Suzi Quatro, Dan Hill, Elvis Costello, the Vibrators, Kate Bush, Earth, Wind and Fire, Gerry Rafferty and the Manhattan Transfer. Plus, dance group Legs and Co. Originally broadcast on March 16, 1978

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Peter Powell introduce the edition first aired on March 24, featuring performances by Sinitta, A-ha, Simon Harris, Wet Wet Wet, Debbie Gibson, Climie Fisher, Iron Maiden, Tiffany, Aswad and Whitney Houston

programme.title: Elkie Brooks: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: Annie Nightingale introduces Elkie Brooks at the Hippodrome, Golders Green in 1977. The concert was originally broadcast as part of the BBC Sight and Sound in Concert series, in which music shows were broadcast simultaneously on TV and radio, allowing viewers to enjoy better sound from their hi-fi speakers

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: How the murder of a pensioner was solved thanks to DNA preserved in her clutched hand. Plus, a sisters fight for justice in the UKs oldest case of double jeopardy

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph visits a property in the Norfolk countryside and in Cheshire, Dion Dublin is in a property where theres hardly any room to move. Plus, a multimillion-pound unit is up for sale in London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Hele near Exeter. Experts Caroline Hawley and John Cameron guide the competing sides as they go in search of the best buys before battling it out at auction in Bristol. Charlie also visits the home of Bristol Blue Glass and learns how glass-making hasnt changed much since the Roman era

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie meets distillery manager Luke in Bristol to explain how swapping his coffee and energy drinks for tea could lower his stress levels, reduce anxiety, calm the brain and support heart health

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Pensioner David has decided to sell his rare and unique circus memorabilia and put the money raised towards a good cause - entertaining locals with his clown and variety act and helping support his poorly wife. After being greeted by a knights suit of armour, auctioneer Christina Trevanion makes museum-quality finds among Davids collection, including items that once belonged to famous circus owner PT Barnum. Meanwhile, JJ Chalmers finds an important and iconic piece of British innovation lying forgotten in a garden shed.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps two retired drama teachers swap Sheffield for a new rural life on the Devon coast. He also explores a 550-acre rewilding project brimming with wildflower meadows

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Will Kirk heads to Bletchley Park, Buckinghamshire, where he helps revive a historic Hollerith card-punching machine that powered Second World War code breaking. Back at the barn, Richard Talman restores a cherished floral brooch

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Robb, Louisa, Niall, Ben and Nkechi race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Rose Matafeo

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Italy v Northern Ireland (Kick-off 7.45pm). Stephen Watson presents all the action from the FIFA World Cup play-off semi-final at Stadio di Bergamo, as the teams compete for the chance to face Wales or Bosnia and Herzegovina for a place in this summers World Cup finals. Given their home advantage and greater experience, the Italians will go into this contest as favourites. However, they did suffer a shock home defeat at the hands of Macedonia at this stage of qualifying for the 2022 tournament, which will give Northern Ireland hope of remaining in contention to qualify for their first World Cup finals since 1986. The nations last met in the group stage of qualifying for 2022, when Italy claimed a 2-0 home win courtesy of goals by Domenico Berardi and Ciro Immobile, before there was a 0-0 stalemate at Windsor Park. With analysis from Stuart Dallas, Chris Brunt, Stephen Craigan and Gianfranco Zola

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the weeks political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience in Clacton-on-Sea, Essex

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts a weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: How criminals making millions selling counterfeit cigarettes were caught hiding their stash in secret drawers. Plus, a drugs courier who claimed to have won £200,000 gambling. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers in Birkenhead in Merseyside, and Ilkeston in Derbyshire, both manage to create additional bedrooms in their properties by rejigging existing layouts. A bungalow in Orpington, south-east London, is also appraised. Presented by Jacqui Joseph, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Shepton Mallet, Somerset as Raj Bisram and Thomas Forrester help two teams find profitable antiques to sell at auction. Friends and neighbours Pat and Linda, lead by Raj Bisram, have sparkly items on their shopping list but are taken by a flamingo-headed walking cane. Sisters Jess and Jo, guided by Thomas Forrester, are looking for something that jumps out at them from the get-go

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Father tries to find Bishop-Elect Fox who, after taking revenge on Father Brown, has been kidnapped before his consecration

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Wanting to be close to the coast, a couple from Birmingham decide that Cornwall is the perfect destination for their escape. Sonali Shah is on hand to make their dream come true. She also visits a company creating habitats for solitary bees that can also be used in the construction of homes for humans too

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: The team is in Clackmannanshire hoping to revive Alloa Co-op Bowling Clubs faded and warped sign in their centenary year. Meanwhile, Dom Chinea delivers a 19th-century regulator clock from the Hebrides with a ghostly connection, and Will Kirk heads north to Rothes in Moray to learn the craft behind forging traditional copper stills for the multibillion-dollar whisky industry

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Sammie, Robert, Caz, Laura and Ivor race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Alliance Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and JB Gill present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: I Tackled a Terrorist With a Narwhal Tusk
programme.desc: Sculptor Nick attempts to create a piece honouring civil servant Darryn, who fought off a knife-wielding terrorist with a narwhal tusk in London in 2019. Bill Bailey believes Nick is the perfect artist to make a sculpture that honours both the hero and the human behind the headlines

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: When the circumstances of a crime novelists death eerily mirror the details of his final novel, Humphrey must uncover whether it was a coincidence or murder by design. The Lily Bond is caught adrift leaving Humphrey and Martha back home with Anne. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The body of Giles, an eccentric monster hunter, is recovered from the sea. He bears injuries oddly suggestive of the legendary creature hes spent years chasing - the Lusca. Mervins life is turned upside down by an unexpected visit from his niece Eloise

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr joins Claudia to talk about series two of Last One Laughing, Chase Infiniti speaks about her role in The Testaments - the sequel to The Handmaids Tale - and Lisa Kudrow talks about sitcom The Comeback. Meanwhile, Jamie Dornan previews Saturday Night Live UK, which he is guest hosting

programme.title: The MOBO Awards
programme.desc: BBC Radio 1 Xtras DJ Target presents exclusive backstage coverage, including all the glitz and glamour of the red carpet, alongside performances and interviews with the likes of Lauryn Hill, Olivia Dean, Myles Smith, Tiwa Savage, Jamaican dancehall star Shenseea and hip-hop legend Slick Rick

programme.title: MOBO at 30: Iconic Moments
programme.desc: Celebrating 30 years of the MOBO Awards, its ambition, cultural impact and legacy, with a compilation of the best moments and memorable performances from the award shows

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall, Samin Nosrat and Vito Coppola, and special guests Rebecca Lucy Taylor and Phil Daniels. Helen McGinn selects the drinks

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the worlds biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Three childish young men try to navigate through the hardships of life. Life is good for the funboys until Callum gets his first-ever girlfriend and the change in dynamic threatens both their friendship - and Jordans life. A burgeoning relationship means that Callum no longer wants to play video games all weekend with the fellas. This proves too much for the others to bear. Comedy, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Dylan

programme.title: Funboys
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Ballymacnoose
programme.desc: Following a gas leak at his flat, Callum moves in with Lorcan and his father Sammy, widely regarded as Ballymacnooses biggest rotter. Driven by a desire to no longer be a pushover and in a desperate attempt to get over his break-up with Gemma, Callum goes under Sammys wing and unleashes his own inner swine. Meanwhile, Jordans two fathers are finally tying the knot and he becomes wrapped up in all the excitement of the wedding. Comedy, with Ryan Dylan and Rian Lennon

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: With Callum now unwelcome at Boggins farm and his flat still out of bounds because of a gas leak, he turns once more to his oldest, dearest friend, Jordan. Things seem to be going well - Jordans two fathers show Callum some much-needed sympathy and introduce him to the foreign concept of talking about his feelings. Unfortunately this new-found bond doesnt go down so well with the other member of the house - Jordan. Meanwhile, Gemma brings home a pet and unwittingly causes Lorcan to relive some very painful memories. Comedy, with Ryan Dylan and Rian Lennon

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Gemma and Lorcan throw a party, but Gemma finds one of her old personality quirks coming to the fore again as she struggles to maintain her identity amidst the intoxicating brew of Lorcans old-fashioned country charm. Callum makes a new friend in Gemmas brother David, but finds theres a catch. Comedy, with Rian Lennon, Ryan Dylan and Ele McKenzie

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Its the day of the election, and Femi prepares to take on Cheddar. With a lot riding on his success, Femi recruits the help of a reluctant Toby, who is dealing with his own crisis. Omar has a daring plan, while Leah is pulled away from Benjamin by a struggling Felix

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.desc: The BBCs gaming reporter Steffan Powell is joined by a host of special guests to consider forgotten franchises, discussing which titles they wish would make a return

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is spat at and bitten by several creatures as he takes a look at venom

programme.title: The Addams Family 2 (2021)
programme.desc: Lawyer Mr Mustela approaches Gomez and Morticia for a sample of their daughter Wednesdays DNA. The advocate claims the enfant terrible was switched in the maternity ward with another child, leaving Gomez devastated at the prospect of losing his little girl. Animated family comedy, with the voices of Oscar Isaac and Charlize Theron and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Divergent (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager grows up in a strictly ordered future society where peoples lives are decided according to personality types. She discovers she does not fit into any of the existing categories, and must fight against a conspiracy to wipe out all those like her. Sci-fi adventure based on the book series by Veronica Roth, starring Shailene Woodley and Kate Winslet

programme.title: Divergent: Insurgent (2015)
programme.desc: An outsider in a strictly regimented society where peoples lives are determined by personality types faces a new crisis as conflict brews between different factions. Her isolation from the general population may make her the only one who can save her world. Sci-fi adventure sequel based on Veronica Roths novel, starring Shailene Woodley and Kate Winslet

programme.title: Divergent: Allegiant (2016)
programme.desc: Rebels fighting for freedom in a strictly ordered society escapes from the walled city they live in. They find themselves in a strange and dangerous new world, where they face a battle for their own survival and of those they have left behind. Third in the sci-fi adventure series based on Veronica Roths novels, starring Shailene Woodley, Theo James and Naomi Watts

programme.title: My Mates a Bad Date
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Khalil from Nottingham cannot get a second date. Oloni thinks that remembering his dates names, dropping the baby girl tag and cooling off on the bragging might help his chances

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBCs world affairs editor reflects on the weeks major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmiths darkly comic anthology returns. Pantalone is planning the heist of the century - a tasty £12million in rough-cut diamonds. But if youre going to hire a bunch of complete clowns to carry out a robbery, and half of them are planning to double-cross the other half, youve not just got a recipe for disaster, youve actually got the ingredients of a Tarantino Commedia dellArte. Starring Kevin Bishop, Gemma Whelan and Paterson Joseph

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya continues her journey through Spain, where she trains with a team of firefighters and learns how the country is spending billions to fight climate change - money that is coming out of the national defence budget. She then travels to France, witnessing a rocket launch in French Guiana, and learning more about the European Space Agency, which has its base in Paris. In the capital, she also finds out about the countrys generous welfare state, before venturing into the countryside to meet farmers who are fed up with European regulation

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: If You Say It Out Loud, Its Real
programme.desc: Hurt by Annas absence, Rachel becomes even more unsettled when Claire accidentally lets something slip. Maggie receives big news of her own and later crosses paths with Luke, who is still shaken by his last encounter with Rachel. At the flat, Anna is caught off guard by an unexpected visit from Janie, who arrives with startling news

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. Zoe may be a beginner but the science is on her side - getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a retired couple from Northamptonshire find their perfect coastal retreat in Devon. Theyre dreaming of a single-storey home close to the sea, and one of the properties delivers breathtaking ocean views right from the terrace. Nicki also meets a couple raising a family in a Dartmoor village, who share their love of the outdoors by mapping walks in the National Park for all ages and abilities to enjoy

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Spring is in full stride at Longmeadow, and Monty Don gets straight to work, bringing new borders to life, reviving winter-worn houseplants and priming his pelargoniums for a standout summer. Joe Swift enjoys access to the private garden of renowned designer Mary Keen, and at RHS Garden Rosemoor, Carol Klein reveals how fruit and veg can deliver both flavour and flair - no matter the size of your plot

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: The six best players come together in the semi-final, beginning with a series of highly tactical game sin which they can select the same piece more than once and position them on the back rank as they wish. The three losers then fight for their place by facing off against nine-year-old chess prodigy Bodhana Sivanandan, the youngest player ever to represent England in an international competition. Presented by Sue Perkins

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive makes a wriggly catch on a fishing expedition across Europes biggest volcanic lake, and discovers how scientists are fighting to save an ancient citadel from destruction

programme.title: Big Little Journeys
programme.desc: The adventures of six tiny animals as they voyage into the unknown, following them as they travel across rainforests, mountains and wetlands. In Canada, a tiny turtle ventures through a forest of giants in search of the wetland, while in South Africa, a bushbaby is drawn towards a world of light in his quest to find a home

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team visits Cliveden in Buckinghamshire, where items valued include a collection of jewellery found on a rubbish tip, a Stanley Spencer sketch and a brooch presented by the Prince of Wales to his tiger-hunting host. Michael Aspel also gets a surprise concerning a champions boxing belt

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts David Barby and David Fletcher appraise antiques at Truro Cathedral, while Paul Martin takes a break from the valuations to discover the Minack Theatre, which was built almost single-handedly by a woman on the Cornish coast

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: In one of Richard Osmans final shows as host, former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine test their general knowledge skills ahead of double points Friday

programme.title: Trace, Track, Get My Car Back
programme.desc: Vehicle recovery specialist Richard is on the trail of a caravan thats been stolen during a late-night raid on a site in Nottingham. Tracker Joe hunts down a stolen Range Rover thats being dismantled for parts in a workshop

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: JB Gill and Clara Amfo present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy finds herself in hot water, Bea orchestrates a plan, and Vicki supports Zack. Penny accepts Vinnys offer of a shop after words of encouragement from Gina and Harry. However, Suki accuses Penny of using Vinny for money

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In their last chance to secure the win, the remaining three chefs are tasked with making the best three-course meal of their lives. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Matt Tebutt are looking for extraordinary levels of technique that deliver flavour combinations to take them to the top and help them make the difficult decision of who should be crowned MasterChef: The Professionals 2026 champion

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Lord Sugar tasks the candidates with selling products on a TV shopping channel. They must select the products they hope will win over the nation and make them the most profit before taking to the small screen. Theres a dramatic showdown in the boardroom and one candidate risks everything to keep their place in the process

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon and guests examine the candidates performances and analyse the TV Selling task, the boardrooms and the firings. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Headline analysis with Patrick OConnell

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: The inside story of how America and China have become locked in a battle for global power and influence since Donald Trump first took office. The documentary begins with Trump inauguration in 2017, when he upended the USs relationship with Beijing. He promised to make China pay - but when he started a trade war, Xi Jinping was ready to fight back

programme.title: Licorice Pizza (2021)
programme.desc: In the San Fernando Valley in 1973, 25-year-old Alana Kane is going through the motions as a photographers assistant when 15-year-old child star Gary Valentine makes romantic overtures toward her. Despite the age gap, she agrees to go to dinner with him. Paul Thomas Andersons romantic comedy, starring Alana Haim, Cooper Hoffman, Sean Penn, Tom Waits and Bradley Cooper

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East England and Yorkshire: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from north east England and Yorkshire compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. The dishes are judged by veteran judge Paul Ainswort, which include a creation using duck, fresh fig and king oyster mushrooms to show what the kestrel in the classic film Kes might see whilst flying over the moors

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: In the wake of a family tragedy, the Bennet household is thrown into disarray. Mary faces an uncertain future, until her aunt and uncle offer her the chance of a fresh start. At first, Mary struggles to adjust to her new life, but she begins to realise that she can have a life away from the watchful eye of her mother

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Will Kirk heads to Bletchley Park, Buckinghamshire, where he helps revive a historic Hollerith card-punching machine that powered Second World War code breaking. Back at the barn, Richard Talman restores a cherished floral brooch

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph is not impressed by the mushrooms growing inside a Norfolk end-of-terrace, while Dion Dublin thinks walls need to be rebuilt in a West Midlands property. Martel Maxwell meets a foster father and son who have teamed up to become developers

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the last semi-final. Four contenders must face five brutal events before going head to head in the legendary Eliminator for a place in next weeks final. Steel has a score to settle in Hang Tough, Viper charges into Suspension Bridge, and Fire enjoys an epic fight with a firefighter

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: The current set of players faces its final day competing for the chance to win £10,000 before 24 new faces arrive. Game show, hosted by Rick Edwards

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: Sue Perkins presents as the four finalists compete for the trophy, facing a challenge to demonstrate how well they can find the checkmate, before the hopefuls are whittled down in a daunting simultaneous match against grandmaster David Howell. The remaining two then step up to the board for the final eliminator, each hoping they can use all the skills they have learnt to make them series champion

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive has visited Rome before, but this time heads out of the centre and up one of the citys seven hills to the rarely visited neighbourhood of Monteverde, where he talks football with a butcher and puts his Caribbean cooking knowledge to good use. He also tries his hand at Italys fastest growing sport, stays in the citys most unusual hotel and has a go at an ancient Roman art form

programme.title: Big Little Journeys
programme.desc: In Taiwan, a Formosan pangolin travels through a land of giants in a protected forest and must prove himself worthy of a mate. In Brazils Atlantic forest, golden-headed lion tamarins travel to the edge of their world and find a strange land of plenty. Will the family find a new home where they can live peacefully alongside humans?

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team returns to Cliveden in Buckinghamshire, where items that came under scrutiny include a delicate satinwood bureau brought in by a descendant of artist Joshua Reynolds, a silver cruet set, and a collection of illustrated letters from India described as the pop art of the 1850s. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Tracy Martin value items at Oxford Universitys Sheldon Theatre, including a Rolex watch and an unwanted inkwell. Paul Martin hears the story behind a book that took 70 years to complete - the Oxford English Dictionary

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine test their general knowledge skills on double points Friday, when the days champion, and the weeks victor is revealed

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: The chef celebrates British food, examining the stories behind the nations best-loved dishes and meeting people involved in providing the ingredients for them, He begins in the Lake District, enjoying a traditional mutton hotpot with sheep farmer and author James Rebank, then braves Morecambe Bays treacherous mud flats to meet a shrimp fisherman. Back home in Padstow, Rick is joined by his son Jack to cook his mums shepherds pie

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East England and Yorkshire: Judging
programme.desc: Its judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent north east England and Yorkshire. Judges Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, and Phil Wang are joined by screenwriter and author Frank Cottrell-Boyce, who helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don is busy with spring and planning ahead for summer. Hes doing a seed-sowing masterclass, cutting back Cornus and dividing ferns to put in the new Woodland Garden. Frances Tophill is exploring the tiny but expansive world of miniature daffodils at a nursery with hundreds of cultivars. And in Llanelli, Sue Kent meets a gardener who has set up her own seed library, helping to preserve varieties of flowers and edibles which flourish in local Welsh soil and beyond

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Reunited to protect themselves from intruding male lions, the Xudum pride mothers now struggle to feed so many mouths. Tsebe has four cubs, and her smallest is close to starving. When he becomes injured and then lost, his life is on a knife edge

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean shares her light-hearted views on modern life in this show recorded before a packed house at Londons Hammersmith Apollo. She also introduces comedians Catherine Bohart, and Babatunde Aleshe to the stage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Femme (2023)
programme.desc: Jules is targeted in a horrific homophobic attack, so when he encounters one of his attackers in a sauna some time later, he wants revenge. Thriller, starring Nathan Stewart-Jarrett, Aaron Heffernan, George MacKay and Nima Taleghani

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Colletta Smith investigates the rising cost-of-living impact for communities as rising prices, wages and higher taxes push businesses and families to the brink. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said that 2026 would be the year that more people would begin to feel positive change, but independent experts say we may have to wait a little longer before we notice a few extra pounds in our pockets
