programme.title: Operation Arctic
programme.desc: Originally broadcast live from the Arctic, this series follows the struggles of polar bears living on this frozen tundra and highlights the impact that environmental changes are having on the bears and the changing world around them. Simon Reeve, Kate Humble and Gordon Buchanan examine the effects of climate change and the struggles these bears now face as the sea ice continues to melt at an alarming rate

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Hunting Outback Gold
programme.desc: In the search for billions of dollars in gold, historian Tanya uncovers a shocking new theory that flips the hunt to the east

programme.desc: Derek picks out a solid-looking 1969 MGB for the team to look out for at auction. After winning it is sent to a father-and-son team to restore

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil finds a pinball machine that should be simple to repair, but just when Rob thinks hes got the flippers flipping, another tricky obstacle gets in the way

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil relives his childhood memories with a classic Scalextric, but its a new challenge for Rob who never owned one as a child and has never fixed one either

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michaels final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex. At Cowley, he marvels at an automated state-of-the-art production line that produces 1,000 Minis a day. Michael probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford and is delighted to find that cars for export are loaded in Italian Job style on to trains at the factorys own railhead

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers and in Henley-on-Thames he slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance for Michael to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore Londons new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a 1970s beige with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: In Kursk, a battlefield the size of Wales, the tide turns, and the Soviets push back, but as Germany retreats, they destroy all evidence of their genocidal activities

programme.title: Churchills Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: The story of how Winston Churchill tried to save France and destroy Hitler by destroying Britain and creating the Franco-British Union - a plan that was to crash and burn

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the grounds of Aston Hall in Birmingham, joined by Benjamin Zephaniah, who shares his passion for unusual banknotes and currency - including a gold medal honouring Ethiopian Emperor Haile Selassie. Items brought in to be valued include a collection of football memorabilia, artwork and mountaineering equipment, and wooden figures depicting characters from The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Dyffryn Gardens near Cardiff, where treasures include a ships chest from the Windrush, an ornate Welsh harp, a clock that runs on air, and a collection of ceramics, including many rarities picked up for just a few pounds. Plus, former Welsh tennis player Elizabeth James shows some of the dresses she wore in the 1960s

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team pays tribute to mid-80s-Midlands by breathing new life into what was once the fastest production MG ever made - the Montego Turbo

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team break with tradition and actually follow Dave Mathewsons advice on what to restore - he points to a 1992 Vauxhall Nova Merit Plus!

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside

programme.title: Restoration Workshop

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil buys a bargain Boombox fresh out of the 80s. It looks amazing but Rob predicts it will be a nightmare to repair

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil picks up an iconic Weltron Space Helmet Radio in need of repair, but the spherical design proves a challenge to even get open

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.sub-title: Air Raid Shelter and Pedal-Powered Washing Machine
programme.desc: The team reinvent a vintage DIY air raid shelter as a hi-tech garden shed. Plus, Jenny and Ellis work on a pedal-powered washing machine.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey across Northern Ireland to Argyll and Bute. Beginning in Newry, he finds a specially chartered train that would deliver demonstrators campaigning for Irish Home Rule to a rally in the town. On the border between Northern Ireland and the Republic at Glaslough, Michael discovers a christening robe belonging to Sir Winston Churchill

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Belfast to Portrush, he investigates the work of author and Narnia creator CS Lewis and learns how religion influenced Lewiss work. Michael also visits the Royal Portrush Golf Club to find out how Irelands female population dominated the fairways at the time of his guide

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Northern Ireland across the water to Scotland. Leaving from the seaport of Larne, he reaches Stranraer and the Mull of Galloway, where in a lighthouse built by railway engineer Robert Stevenson he discovers a magnificent machine, installed at the turn of the 20th-century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Glasgow to Cumbrae, with his early 20th-century Bradshaws guide in hand, he is put to work behind the scenes at Glasgows circular subway. He also explores the future of shipbuilding on the Clyde and hears how one woman led a successful mass protest against high rents in the citys notorious tenements

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: In the 100th episode, Derek heads to Northumberland to collect a rare 1975 NSU RO 80, Jack deals with a Mini Mayfair stuck in a barn, and an Aston Martin DBS husk is auctioned

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: On the way to pick up two lovely 70s Lotus sports cars, Derek takes to the skies for his first ever helicopter flight in a 1967 Bell 47 chopper

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: The Red Army develops strength and Hitlers Spring Offensive is stopped at Stalingrad; and in the largest ghetto the inhabitants fight back in the Warsaw Uprising

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: In 1985, a very tense time, a young Mikhail Gorbachev is instated as the new leader of the Soviet Union. East and West have been publicly stockpiling nuclear weapons for decades, neither side wanting to appear weak. Plus, the spying game threatens the first tentative moves towards peace

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Bishops Palace in Wells, Somerset. Items brought in to be valued include a Delft plaque from a Bristol pub, decorated with a drinking song by composer Purcell, which could be of national importance, a portrait of an unknown First World War army officer, and a collection of movie memorabilia, including rare posters. Plus, Basic, Better and Best features a range of items by Finnish designers

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh. Finds include a set of traditional Highland dress that once belonged to a clan chieftain, a selection of medals awarded to a pioneering diver, a silver trophy in the shape of a rams head, and a 19th-century bust by sculptor Thomas Stuart Burnett. Fiona gets the chance to take a closer look at a plant specimen collected by Charles Darwin during his voyage on HMS Beagle, and is challenged to put a selection of ceramic piglets by the Wemyss firm in order of value

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a solid-looking 1969 MGB for the team to look out for at auction. After winning it is sent to a father-and-son team to restore

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Dave alerts the team to a 1979 Lotus Esprit S3. Its an expensive buy and a serious risk, but its also a thoroughbred classic in need of a proper recommission

programme.title: Operation Arctic
programme.desc: Originally broadcast live from the Arctic, this series follows the struggles of polar bears living on this frozen tundra and highlights the impact that environmental changes are having on the bears and the changing world around them. Simon Reeve, Kate Humble and Gordon Buchanan examine the effects of climate change and the struggles these bears now face as the sea ice continues to melt at an alarming rate

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Hunting Outback Gold
programme.desc: In the search for billions of dollars in gold, historian Tanya uncovers a shocking new theory that flips the hunt to the east

programme.desc: Shamil finds a pinball machine that should be simple to repair, but just when Rob thinks hes got the flippers flipping, another tricky obstacle gets in the way

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil relives his childhood memories with a classic Scalextric, but its a new challenge for Rob who never owned one as a child and has never fixed one either

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michaels final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex. At Cowley, he marvels at an automated state-of-the-art production line that produces 1,000 Minis a day. Michael probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford and is delighted to find that cars for export are loaded in Italian Job style on to trains at the factorys own railhead

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers and in Henley-on-Thames he slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance for Michael to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore Londons new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a 1970s beige with 36,000 miles on the clock

Medical drama, starring Paul Fox

programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: The show returns to London, where roofer Bruce, Essex boy Lewis and physiotherapist Mirkan all hope to impress marketing manager Leila with their style choices. Whoever dresses her the best will get to join her for a romantic first date, but which lucky fella will it be?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Michael Jackson impersonator Mitch, bar manager Aiden and student JJ try to impress Becky, a student from Leeds, with their outfit choices

programme.desc: Famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Tonight, the remaining four celebrities present their best Thai-inspired dishes as they battle to stay in the competition. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.desc: Labrador pup Bonnie must undergo life-saving heart surgery, while ragdoll cat owner John needs to uncover what is wrong with his favourite feline

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans its an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the animals that thrive in inner-city environments across the country. Urban foxes did not exist in Britain until the 1930s, but now these animals inhabit 90% of towns and cities and take residence in built-up living quarters. Meanwhile, cameras observe families of hedgehogs thriving in unkempt gardens, and peregrine falcons nesting in some on Londons tallest buildings

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Tati and her team don hazmat gear to clean a filthy home overrun by pets. Window cleaners battle it out for employee of the month and Dublin Citys night crew scrub the streets

programme.title: The Girl Before
programme.desc: Drama about a woman who is given the chance to move into a beautiful, ultra-minimalist house designed by an enigmatic architect. Theres just one catch - occupants have to abide by his list of exacting rules, and very soon she feels the house changing her in unexpected ways. Starring Gugu Mbatha-Raw, David Oyelowo, Jessica Plummer and Ben Hardy

programme.title: Living with Lucy
programme.desc: Presenter Lucy Kennedy moves in with Jedward for 48 hours, as the duo stay in Dublin to prepare for the glamorous IFTA Awards

programme.title: Cracker
programme.sub-title: To Say I Love You - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. Fitz is arrested for causing a breach of the peace while trying to speak to Judith, and ends up helping the police track down a couple on a murderous rampage after being asked to speak with a man arrested for stealing a mini-bus. Hard-hitting drama, starring Robbie Coltrane and Christopher Eccleston, with Andrew Tiernan and Susan Lynch

programme.title: Time Is Money

programme.desc: Colette Fitzpatrick talks to Sligo brother and sister Jessie and Christopher Farrell, two of five siblings abused and neglected by their father, Noel Farrell, over a period of 20 years

programme.title: The Rock (1996)
programme.desc: A renegade general and his crack team of mercenaries take over the abandoned fortress prison of Alcatraz and threaten San Francisco with toxic nerve-gas rockets to extort a massive ransom. To break their stranglehold on the city, the FBI sends in a chemical weapons expert and a jailed SAS veteran, chosen because he is the only man to have ever escaped from the infamous penitentiary. Action thriller, with Sean Connery, Nicolas Cage and Ed Harris

programme.title: Law and Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A high-school principal catches a pupil sexting and the team uncovers a sinister case of distribution concerning hundreds of underage students. Silva pleads for leniency for a suspect she believes is innocent

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A chilling event at the Royal Edward hotel on the night of Assistant Chief Constable Alison Vospers leaving do forces Grace and Branson into a difficult position. They must investigate all guests, including high ranking East Sussex police attendees. Something about the incident is causing a deep unease in Grace - he knows hes seen this before. Has a sinister offender from the crime busters past resurfaced or is this a copycat? Return of the crime drama based on the books by Peter James, starring John Simm

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, with Nadine Coyle, Rick Edwards and Jake Wood guessing well-known phrases for the chance to win £50,000 for good causes

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: John and Mehreen recover from their stand-off with Asadi and the Stockport crime gang and with MI5 now finally on his side, John tracks the general to an upmarket hotel. An unexpected lead from his informant Jordan gives the breakthrough hes been looking for, but he is stunned by a shocking revelation from Mehreen before he can alert his colleagues

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Alex Brooker and his mum Elaine, Jeff Brazier and his son Bobby, and Sophie Ellis-Bextor and her mum Janet Ellis compete in the comedy game show. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms. From December 2016

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, detailing events from the desperate 14-day police search to the discovery of their bodies

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of more hotels and BandBs around Britain, beginning with a visit to the Rosehill House Hotel in Burnley, Lancashire. Owned for the past 25 years by John and run by his daughter Emma, they have cluttered the 31-room establishment with tat and lost sight of who their customers are. Recently, bookings have taken a sharp decline and to make matters worse Johns wife Jacquie - once the life and soul of the hotel - has been forced to retire due to illness, leaving Emma to run the place alone

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: Michael Palin in Nigeria
programme.desc: In the last leg of his journey, Michael heads to the city of Benin, where he learns about the 1897 Raid on Benin, viewing a commemorative statue and visiting Igun Street to see bronze statues being made. Onboard a boat on the River Ethiope, he meets Nigerian filmmaker Chuko Esiri. They discuss Nigerias wildlife and the inspiration that Chuko gets from this location

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines more real-life cases, beginning with the 2001 murder of 15-year-old Danielle Jones, who went missing on her way to school

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is viciously attacked while attending a broken-down vehicle stranded on the moors. When the body of the driver is discovered nearby, Rob and Rachel are determined to solve the mystery. Their investigation leads to a chilling discovery linking the case to a religious cult near Aidensfield

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie, Stephanie, and Abigail help Martha throw Claire a baby shower that sparks memories of Cassies own. Catherine Bell and James Denton stars

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into the construction of the cutting-edge Ford class aircraft carriers, the largest warships ever built, which are almost as long as the Empire State Building is tall

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Bob, Susan and Selina want to see more of rural Rajasthan and head into the hills to go horse riding and camping. Stanley the adventurer relives his youth on the back of a motorcycle, and Syd and Stephanie explore their inner selves through meditation. Later, a sleeper train takes the group to Navratri, one of Indias biggest annual festivals. After attempting to learn a Gujarati folk dance, they get kitted out in traditional costumes and join thousands of worshippers for a night of celebrations for the powerful Hindu warrior goddess Durga

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade investigates the mysterious deaths of fishermen in Alaska. A recent fatal incident sends him on a search for possible suspects - first through treacherous rapids, then down to the river-mouth where the icy waters are swarming with sharks. Yet its an unexpected beast, hauled up from the depths, that eventually emerges as his deadliest contender

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie drives into the mountains to help with a case that reminds her that being a vet is anything but glamorous, as she has to lance a giant boil the size of a watermelon on the leg of a cow. Julie, Castle Vets head nurse, has seen most things in her long career, but today is a first for her, as a rare wild animal has been brought in, found lying at the side of the road and before assessing whats wrong with it, she must first find out what it is

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kates ex-boyfriend turns up in Aidensfield and finds himself under suspicion for a hit-and-run accident. Meanwhile, Bellamy finds himself in charge of organising a local talent contest. Rural police drama, starring Niamh Cusack and Mark Jordon

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From the Grapefruit to the Skinny Gut Diet
programme.desc: Lauren and Robyn from Watford have 12 days to slim down before a 30th birthday party, with one eating half a grapefruit before each calorie-controlled meal and the other going on the apple cider vinegar diet. Two mums from north London want to lose weight for a friends daughters bat mitzvah in six weeks time, so Emma goes on a regimen to balance her gut bacteria, while Elisa takes on the blood-focused Eat Right for Your Type diet, and Carl and Paul in Wigan go on raw and fast food diets. Xand also sheds light on some of the latest dieting trends and diet obsessions, including DIY enemas

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to the Walpole Bay Hotel, a family-run establishment in Margate, Kent, that was first featured on the programme in 2009. On her original visit, she criticised owner Jane Bishop for not giving her grown-up children enough of a say in the business - and was far from impressed with the collection of bizarre trinkets that decorated the hotels corridors. Three years on, she returns to see if the Bishops have been able to stick to her guidelines, or if Janes bad habits have returned

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is concerned, and when Cain has a fit of rage, she tries to reassure him

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo stuns Todd by suggesting they get married right away, Bernie experiences divided loyalties to her son and husband, and Will struggles with being the focus of local gossip

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Adam is quick to question Dr Weatherills competence when a beach outing ends tragically for a family. Dr Ormerod leaps in to defend her - but ends up revealing his colleagues condition in his haste. Meanwhile, Dr Goodwin struggles to help a pair of teenage parents gain access to their baby. Amy Robbins and Robert Daws star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A grandmother is suspected of snatching a baby from outside a shop following a family quarrel, but Kate believes one of her patients may be responsible. Meanwhile, Greengrass buys a racehorse and begins to train it on the streets of Aidensfield - much to the dismay of Sergeant Blaketon. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Murder By Mushroom
programme.desc: The case that gripped Australians and many people around the world, after Erin Patterson invited dinner guests into her home and poisoned them with toxic death cap mushrooms slipped into individual beef Wellingtons. Three guests died, while another was seriously injured

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bernie pays Mal a visit in his hotel room, George gains an insight into Theos treatment of Todd, Ben feels guilty for letting Will down, and Jodie offers Carla her childminding services

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert is accused, Joe is stunned, while Chas and Kim form an unlikely bond

programme.title: Cracker
programme.desc: Part two of two. Fitz takes a desperate gamble by confronting the murderous Janice, who has kidnapped his son Mark and spirited him away to a secret location. While he is risking everything to resolve the case, wife Judith contemplates an affair - with his brother Danny. Robbie Coltrane stars, with Barbara Flynn, Geraldine Somerville and Emily Joyce

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: As the siege drags on in Tobruk, Harry leads a mission to find a source of drinking water. While they are successful in finding a well, tragedy strikes when they are caught out by a German ambush. In Manchester, Kasia is confronted by further horror of war when she discovers Irenas motives for spying for the Nazis. In France, romance blossoms between David and Henriette as they hide out in a farm

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Claudia Lawrence, a York chef who disappeared in March 2009, and Len Saunders, a 65-year-old actor who was killed in a one-punch attack

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Reverend Jacob Thwaite returns home to find his wife has been murdered, but while Rachel is suspicious of his alibi its a psychic convention in the town that seems to hold the key to the case. Meanwhile, Peggy devises a plan to sell trout to a hotel in Scarborough that lands her and David in trouble with the authorities. Trudie Goodwin guests

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Sam is excited to perform surgery on a star basketball player. Joy makes a discovery and decides to revive the Middleton Exchange tradition. Catherine Bell and James Denton star

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The InterContinental Shanghai Wonderland, a 337-room hotel that burrows 16 stories below ground level, clinging to the cliffs of an abandoned quarry

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: After a month of testing the waters to see whether they could live a life of retirement in India, the group enjoy their final week at the Udaipur Haveli. Susan, Syd and the Krankies go on one last adventure as they head off to the pilgrimage town of Pushkar, and Bob delves into the world of natural medicine, and his first Ayurvedic treatment comes as rather a surprise. Elsewhere, Stanley organizes a final cricket match between the senior citizens and their new local friends, before the group reflect on friendships formed, and lessons learnt, at the end of a wonderful month spent in Rajasthan

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In Fijis South Pacific paradise, people are coming under attack. Plunging off the beaten track into the remote mountains of the islands interior, freshwater detective Jeremy battles tropical storms and unforgiving jungles in an all-out search for victims, clues, and culprits. Colourful stories of monstrous fish are woven into the local folklore, but how much of this is fanciful, and how much of it is real?

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: As winter morphs into spring, lambing and calving keeps the team busy out on the farms. However, their small animal clinic is also packed with pets and Martin Fitzpatrick - the man who founded the practice 35 years ago - faces a difficult case concerning a Highland terrier. Young vet Darren is called out on an emergency to a farm where a prize cow is struggling to deliver a calf. An examination reveals its simply too big to come out naturally. Darren decides the safest option, for both the cow and the calf, is to perform an emergency Caesarean section. Its a risky operation, but without it, the cow will die

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A grandmother is suspected of snatching a baby from outside a shop following a family quarrel, but Kate believes one of her patients may be responsible. Meanwhile, Greengrass buys a racehorse and begins to train it on the streets of Aidensfield - much to the dismay of Sergeant Blaketon. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From the High-Fat to the Cookie and Paleo diets
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help six more people lose weight with different methods. Natasha and Jodian have just two weeks to slim down before a trip to Egypt, with one trying out a high-fat diet and the other eating Mediterranean food. In Hampshire, Mike tries out a regimen that consists almost entirely of specially-made cookies and Steve is on a volumetric diet consisting of large bowls of soup or massive salads, while Gina and Max in Wigan go on paleolithic and starch diets respectively. Xand also investigates the Ken diet, which involves a protein solution being fed straight into the stomach

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to the African Queen, an eight-bedroom floating hotel on the River Thames, three years after she first tried to help owners Andy and Bonny Cowley revive its fortunes. The couple had previously run a successful hotel on the Isle of Wight, but their new venture was struggling to attract guests - a problem Alex attributed to some uninspiring marketing and the boats far-from-shipshape appearance. A makeover and grand relaunch party seemed to mark a turning point in its fortunes, and Alex now finds out if Andy and Bonny have managed to keep up the good work

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert is accused, Joe is stunned, while Chas and Kim form an unlikely bond

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bernie pays Mal a visit in his hotel room, George gains an insight into Theos treatment of Todd, Ben feels guilty for letting Will down, and Jodie offers Carla her childminding services

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Mr Rose and Dr Ormerod attempt to rescue a drowning boy during a trip to a hippie commune. Catherine is asked on a date by a local fisherman, while Dr Goodwin discovers the parents of a sick baby are not all they seem after he diagnoses the infants illness. Robert Daws, Denis Lill, Amelia Curtis and Paul Fox star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.sub-title: The Things We Do for Love
programme.desc: The discovery of a womans body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victims electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is eager to halt a crimewave before the Assistant Chief Constable arrives for a shoot on Lord Ashfordlys estate, while Kate is faced with a medical and moral dilemma. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: The submarine is forced into lockdown as disaster strikes. With hostile boats tracking and communications down, Vigils crew have to navigate this crisis alone. Kirsten desperately hunts for clues to reveal the source of the threat to Vigil, but with no word from the boat, she fears for the safety of Amy, whose quest to uncover the truth places her in grave danger

programme.title: Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Concluding part. Tennisons trip to Bosnia pays dividends as she makes an exciting breakthrough _ but her determination to find the ruthless killer receives a blow when she returns home to find her chief suspect has friends in very high places, thanks to information he has provided on other war criminals. Undaunted, she vows to see justice done - but arresting the man she believes could be responsible for countless atrocities can only lead to further trouble. With Helen Mirren, Ben Miles, Robert Pugh, Mark Strong and Velibor Topic

programme.title: Vera, Farewell Pet
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes to examine the appeal of ITV detective drama Vera as it comes to an end after 13 years. Brenda Blethyn reflects on her time as DCI Vera Stanhope, and talks about the camaraderie and joy of working on the show. Featuring interviews from key cast members - David Leon (DS Joe Ashworth), Jon Morrison (DC Kenny Lockhart), the directors, crew, and author Ann Cleeves, Veras creator

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Rolandas Poskus, a man who was found dead in a park in Hull, and Amanda Playle, a woman who was stalked by her ex-boyfriend

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield police are puzzled when it seems a face from the past has become the unlikely victim of vandalism, and suspicions are soon aroused over the reasons for his sudden return to the village. Meanwhile, Vernon encounters dangerous wildlife as he stashes his capital in Ashfordly woods, but decides to turn the incident into another money-making scheme. Geoffrey Hughes stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton faces a race against time to stop a saboteur when Aidensfield hosts a prestigious car hill-climb. Meanwhile, Bernie finds an old car in a cowshed and decides to renovate it, giving Vernon his chance to scoop the winnings in the event. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Peter Benson and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A seemingly straightforward burglary investigation takes an unexpected turn when the victim is found to be leading a double life and suspicions are raised that the break-in could have been part of an insurance fraud. Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: Vardy v Rooney: A Courtroom Drama
programme.desc: Dramatic recreation of the sensational Wagatha Christie trial - one of the most extraordinary libel cases ever to be brought before the High Court as two footballers wives came to blows, with Rebekah Vardy trying to sue Coleen Rooney for defamation. The origins of the dispute lay in a 2019 tweet in which Rooney publicly accused Vardy of selling stories to the press about her. With Natalia Tena, Chanel Cresswell and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: The murder of a white prisoner by an Asian inmate creates a media frenzy, while local MP Jo Porter is nowhere to be seen. She eventually stumbles into work to reveal Scott went home with Kirsty at the previous nights party, and Ashika gets her hands on information that could ruin Georges career

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Helena, Andy, Carly and Tony vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Julie from Chesterfield, Aaron from Great Yarmouth, Elaine from Woking and Michael originally from New York pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Hannah, John, Helen and Vince vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.desc: Three suitors buy an outfit for personal stylist Zoe, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes they liked best

programme.title: Hot Cakes
programme.desc: The shop is visited by the current Miss Wales comes in for a cake to present to the winner of this years event. Its a blast from the past for Gareth as they were both professional models at the same agency. As Gareth and Ryan approach their five-year anniversary, Ryans brave enough to arrange a secret surprise for Gareth on a staff night out

programme.title: Live UEFA U21 European Qualifiers
programme.desc: Republic of Ireland v Moldova (Kick-off 5.15pm). All the action from the Group D match at Tallaght Stadium. The Republic are looking to bounce back from a humiliating 4-0 defeat away to Andorra last time out and maintain their chances of qualifying. The reverse fixture in September saw Jim Crawfords side claim a 2-1 win thanks to a brace by Mason Melia

programme.title: Live UEFA European Qualifiers
programme.desc: Denmark v North Macedonia (Kick-off 7.45pm). All the action from the play-off semi-final at Parken Stadium in Copenhagen. The winner of this tie will take on either Republic of Ireland or Czechia away from home for a place in this summers finals in Mexico and USA. The Danes will go into this contest as favourites given their home advantage, but their opponents claimed a shock victory over Italy at this stage of qualifying for the 2022 tournament

programme.title: Dark Side of the 90s
programme.desc: A look at some of the darkest parts of 1990s pop culture. How comedian and actor Arsenio Hall carved out a niche in the predominantly white late-night world by showcasing hip-hop artists and emerging talent before becoming a casualty of the late-night wars

programme.title: Shergar: The Racehorse and the IRA
programme.desc: Set against the backdrop of IRA activity, this documentary investigates the extraordinary 1983 kidnapping of Shergar, a Derby-winning racehorse and global icon

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Katie plans a divorce party trip to Las Vegas and Lake Havasu but conveniently leaves Scheana off the guest list. Lala and Raquel find themselves flirting with the same guy

programme.title: Vardy v Rooney: A Courtroom Drama
programme.desc: Part two of two. Coleens cross-examination begins, as Tomlinson throws doubt upon the legitimacy behind Coleens claims that Rebekah leaked information about her to the press. Coleen rejects the notion that she revelled in celebratory social media memes after her post went viral, while the Wags husbands Wayne and Jamie have a dispute to settle of their own. With Natalia Tena, Chanel Cresswell and Michael Sheen

programme.desc: The pressures of work take their toll on Jo and she turns to drink for comfort. programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Uruguay (Kick-off 7.45pm). Coverage of the friendly at Wembley Stadium, as both teams continue their preparations for this summers FIFA World Cup finals.
